title: Dialogue Boundary Card
description: A compact structure for separating speaker, recipient, visible context, internal context, forbidden context, leakage risks, and instruction drift risks before generating final text.

speaker.description: The voice or role that final text should use.

recipient.description: The person, audience, tool, or downstream agent that will receive the final text.

output_type.description: The output contract that determines what may appear in the final text.

goal.description: The intended outcome of the final text.

visible_context.description: Facts and context the recipient is allowed to see.

internal_context.description: Upstream instructions, negotiations, corrections, or reasoning that must stay internal.

forbidden_context.description: Specific content that must not appear in the final output.

tone.description: The desired tone as it should appear to the recipient, without exposing upstream tone negotiation.

must_include.description: Recipient-visible points that must be included.

must_not_include.description: Points, phrases, or layers that must be excluded.

leakage_risks.description: Boundary failure risks to check before returning final text.

instruction_drift_risks.description: Risks that a generated instruction, skill, policy, or task has drifted from the original concept.
